seo.title: Legal Data Extraction AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for data extraction AI agents in legal. Covers Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules, Court filing standards.

content.intro: Deploying AI agents for legal data extraction carries extraordinary risk: a single hallucinated case citation or improperly handled privileged document can trigger malpractice liability, bar sanctions, or case dismissal as established in Mata v. Avianca. This checklist operationalizes the specific controls required to extract structured legal data safely, ensuring every output meets attorney review requirements, eDiscovery standards, and court filing integrity rules. Use it as a pre-deployment gate and ongoing audit framework for any LLM-powered extraction pipeline touching legal documents.

sections.heading: Data Ingestion & Privilege Protection
sections.description: Controls to ensure documents entering the extraction pipeline are handled in compliance with attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine, and client confidentiality obligations before any LLM processing occurs.

items.label: Privilege Classification Pre-Screen
items.description: Implement an automated privilege tagger that flags documents containing attorney-client communications, work product, or litigation hold markers before they reach the LLM extraction layer. Flagged documents must require explicit attorney authorization to process.

items.label: Client Data Isolation by Matter
items.description: Enforce matter-level data segregation so that documents from Client A's matter are never co-mingled in the same extraction batch or vector store as Client B's documents. Use unique matter IDs as namespace keys in all storage and retrieval systems.

items.label: Model Training Opt-Out Enforcement
items.description: Verify via API contract and technical controls that the LLM provider cannot use any submitted legal documents for model training or fine-tuning. Obtain written confirmation and audit provider data handling policies quarterly.

items.label: Document Provenance Logging
items.description: Log the source file hash, custodian, date received, and matter number for every document ingested. This chain of custody log must be immutable and retained for the duration of the matter plus applicable statute of limitations.

items.label: PII and Sensitive Identifier Redaction Audit
items.description: Before extraction, run a PII detection pass to identify SSNs, financial account numbers, and medical identifiers. Confirm redaction or masking is applied to fields not required by the extraction schema.

items.label: Litigation Hold Compatibility Check
items.description: Confirm that the ingestion pipeline preserves original document metadata (creation date, author, modification history) required by FRCP Rule 34 and does not alter ESI in ways that could constitute spoliation.

items.label: Third-Party Vendor Data Processing Agreement
items.description: Execute a signed Data Processing Agreement (DPA) with every LLM API vendor specifying that legal documents are confidential client data, processing is limited to the contracted extraction task, and breach notification timelines comply with applicable state bar ethics rules.

sections.heading: Schema-Guided Extraction Accuracy
sections.description: Technical controls ensuring the structured output schema produces accurate, complete, and type-consistent fields from unstructured legal documents, with specific attention to citation integrity and legal terminology precision.

items.label: Citation Existence Verification Step
items.description: After extraction, run every extracted case citation (case name, reporter, volume, page) through a verified legal database lookup (Westlaw, Lexis, CourtListener) before the output is accepted. Any citation that returns no match must be flagged as unverified and blocked from downstream use.

items.label: Schema Compliance Validation on Every Output
items.description: Enforce strict JSON schema validation (e.g., using Pydantic or JSON Schema Draft-07) on every LLM response before it is written to the output store. Reject and re-prompt any response that fails required field presence, type constraints, or enum value checks.

items.label: Field-Level Confidence Scoring
items.description: Require the extraction model to output a confidence score (0–1) for each extracted field alongside the value. Fields below a configurable threshold (default 0.85) must be routed to the attorney review queue rather than auto-accepted.

items.label: Completeness Validation Against Document Inventory
items.description: After batch extraction, compare the number of populated required fields against the expected document inventory. Flag any document where more than 10% of required schema fields are null or missing for manual review.

items.label: Legal Date and Deadline Field Accuracy Check
items.description: For fields containing statute of limitations dates, filing deadlines, or hearing dates, implement a secondary validation pass that cross-references extracted dates against the source document text using exact string matching. Mismatches trigger an immediate attorney alert.

items.label: Monetary Amount and Damages Field Reconciliation
items.description: Extracted monetary values must be reconciled against source document figures using a numeric extraction parser independent of the LLM. Discrepancies greater than $0.01 must be flagged for human review before use in any filing or demand letter.

items.label: Party Name Normalization and Disambiguation
items.description: Apply entity resolution to extracted party names to detect variations (e.g., 'Smith Corp.' vs. 'Smith Corporation') and flag potential misidentification of parties, which could result in incorrect service of process or filing errors.

items.label: Extraction Prompt Version Pinning
items.description: Pin the exact prompt template version, model version, and temperature setting used for each extraction run in the audit log. Any change to these parameters must trigger a re-validation run on a held-out test set of 50+ labeled legal documents before production deployment.

sections.heading: Attorney Review & Oversight Workflow
sections.description: Process controls ensuring that licensed attorneys maintain meaningful supervisory control over AI-extracted legal content, satisfying bar association ethics rules on competence, supervision, and unauthorized practice of law.

items.label: Mandatory Attorney Sign-Off Gate for Court Filings
items.description: Implement a hard workflow gate that prevents any AI-extracted content from being inserted into court filings, demand letters, or client-facing legal documents without a licensed attorney's explicit digital approval. The approval must be logged with attorney bar number and timestamp.

items.label: Attorney Review Queue with SLA Tracking
items.description: Build a review queue dashboard that shows all extraction outputs pending attorney review, sorted by filing deadline proximity. Alert supervising attorneys when items have been pending for more than 24 hours or when a deadline is within 72 hours.

items.label: AI Disclosure Notation in Work Product
items.description: Automatically append a disclosure notation to any document containing AI-extracted content indicating that AI tools were used in preparation, consistent with emerging bar guidance (e.g., NY State Bar AI Ethics Opinion 2024). The notation must be removable only by an authorized attorney.

items.label: Supervision Competency Verification
items.description: Require attorneys supervising AI extraction outputs to complete a documented training module on the specific extraction system's error modes, hallucination risks, and review procedures before being granted approval authority in the workflow system.

items.label: Disagreement and Override Logging
items.description: When an attorney modifies or rejects an AI-extracted field, log the original AI output, the attorney's correction, and a required reason code. Aggregate these corrections weekly to identify systematic extraction errors requiring prompt or schema updates.

items.label: Conflict of Interest Screen Before Processing
items.description: Before ingesting any new matter's documents, run an automated conflict check against the firm's existing client and adverse party database. Block processing and alert the conflicts team if a potential conflict is detected.

items.label: Unauthorized Practice of Law (UPL) Boundary Enforcement
items.description: Configure the extraction system to output only factual data fields (dates, names, amounts, document types) and explicitly prohibit the system from generating legal conclusions, advice, or strategy recommendations without attorney authorship.

sections.heading: eDiscovery Compliance & Audit Readiness
sections.description: Controls ensuring the extraction pipeline meets FRCP eDiscovery obligations, produces defensible audit trails, and can respond to opposing counsel challenges or court inquiries about AI-assisted document review processes.

items.label: FRCP Rule 26(g) Defensibility Documentation
items.description: Maintain a written methodology document describing the AI extraction process, model used, validation steps, and attorney review procedures. This document must be producible to opposing counsel or the court upon request as part of eDiscovery protocol disclosures.

items.label: Extraction Run Audit Log Retention
items.description: Retain immutable audit logs of every extraction run including: input document hash, model version, prompt hash, raw output, validation results, and attorney reviewer ID. Logs must be retained for the matter duration plus 7 years per standard legal records retention requirements.

items.label: Technology-Assisted Review (TAR) Protocol Alignment
items.description: If the extraction pipeline is used in eDiscovery document review, document the TAR protocol including seed set selection, quality control sampling rate (minimum 95% confidence, 2% margin of error), and validation methodology to meet court-accepted TAR standards.

items.label: Opposing Counsel Notification Procedure
items.description: Establish a written procedure for notifying opposing counsel of AI tool use in document review when required by local court rules or standing orders (e.g., SDNY AI Standing Order). Automate detection of matters in jurisdictions with mandatory AI disclosure requirements.

items.label: Error Rate Monitoring and Reporting
items.description: Calculate and log the extraction error rate per document type on a weekly basis. If the error rate for any document type exceeds 5%, automatically pause extraction for that document type and require a prompt engineering review before resuming.

items.label: Data Breach and Inadvertent Disclosure Response Plan
items.description: Define and test a documented incident response plan for scenarios where privileged documents are inadvertently processed or disclosed through the AI system, including clawback procedures under FRE 502(d) and state bar notification timelines.

items.label: Extraction Output Reproducibility Test
items.description: Quarterly, re-run a sample of 25 previously extracted documents through the current pipeline and compare outputs to the original logged results. Document any drift in extraction behavior and assess whether prior outputs remain defensible.

items.label: Court-Specific Filing Format Validation
items.description: For extracted data destined for court filings, validate output fields against the specific court's CM/ECF formatting requirements, local rules, and any standing orders regarding AI-generated content before the attorney review gate.

regulatoryNotes.heading: Regulatory Considerations
notes: Under ABA Model Rule 5.3 and state equivalents, supervising attorneys bear direct responsibility for AI-extracted outputs used in legal work product. The firm must implement supervision protocols sufficient to ensure the AI system's conduct would comply with the Rules of Professional Conduct if performed by a human — this means attorney review is not optional but a professional obligation.

The Mata v. Avianca (S.D.N.Y. 2023) sanctions ruling established that submitting AI-generated citations to a court without independent verification constitutes sanctionable conduct. Every citation extracted by the AI system must be verified against a primary legal database before use in any filing, brief, or correspondence, and the verification step must be logged as evidence of due diligence.

Client confidential information is protected under ABA Model Rule 1.6 and cannot be used to train, fine-tune, or improve any AI model without informed client consent. Firms must obtain contractual guarantees from all LLM vendors that submitted data is not retained for training purposes, and must conduct annual vendor audits to verify compliance with this prohibition.

FRCP Rules 26 and 34 govern eDiscovery obligations for electronically stored information (ESI). AI extraction pipelines that process ESI must preserve original metadata, maintain chain of custody documentation, and be capable of producing a defensible methodology statement. Failure to meet these standards can result in adverse inference instructions, evidence preclusion, or sanctions under FRCP Rule 37(e) for ESI spoliation.

cta.heading: Automate Your Legal Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your legal AI extraction pipeline against this checklist — catching privilege violations, citation hallucinations, and schema failures before they reach attorney review or the courtroom.
